item.title: Anecdotal definitions of success
item.description: The definition of success keeps changing as you progress in life. Something we should all be comfortable with.

item.description: I have never watched the AMC show Halt and Catch Fire, and for a long time I only knew the title, but nothing about the show. Something about it always reminded...

item.title: Products are out, brains are in
item.description:
Often, something that should be free is expensive. Ex: bottled water, pre-washed lettuce, filing your taxes, and a bunch more stuff.

item.title: Introducing jjw: a workspace manager for jj
item.description: Introducing jjw, a small Go CLI for managing jj workspaces with bookmarks and lifecycle hooks, built to make parallel LLM coding agent workflows easier.

item.description: Navigating random sidequests

item.title: You don’t know HTML Lists
item.description: This second installment in the "You don't know HTML" series is going to be all about the ways that we put collections of things together

item.title: Wrapping a C library in RAII: unique_ptr with custom deleters and why Subsystem can't be moveable
item.description: I got into the habit of wrapping C libraries before I start working with them. Not out of principle, but because I have seen the same mistakes often enough.

item.title: My mental model of learning
item.description: A mental model for how learning can both constrain and expand creativity.

item.title: How I use LLMs as a staff engineer in 2026
item.description: A bit over a year ago I wrote How I use LLMs as a staff engineer. Here’s a brief summary of what I used AI for last year:

item.title: What breaks when you ship Next.js on Cloudflare Workers
item.description: A log of which npm packages don't survive the Workers runtime, what to swap them with, and the patterns that ended up mattering.

item.title: Fecal transplants for autism deliver success in clinical trials
item.description: Scientific research continues to uncover interesting connections between the gut microbiome and human health, including everything from depression to PTSD to autoimmune disease. Another example of this are emerging ties between gut health and autism. Exciting new research, now moving to Phase 3…

item.title: Moving away from Tailwind, and learning to structure my CSS
item.description: Moving away from Tailwind, and learning to structure my CSS

item.title: The CTF scene is dead
item.description: Why frontier AI has broken the open CTF format, hollowed out the scoreboard, and made competitive CTF performance a weaker signal than it used to be.

item.title: Malloc is NOT Magic: Let's Build it to Learn What's Inside!
item.description: Every program you've ever written or run uses malloc behind the scenes; find out how it works and why it only akin to magic!
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Six SQL patterns I use to catch transaction fraud
item.description: The actual queries I run when I'm hunting fraud in transaction data. Velocity, impossible distances, suspicious amounts, merchant clusters, off-hours buys, and window functions.

item.title: A History of IDEs at Google
item.description: Laurent Le Brun's blog

item.title: DeepSeek-V4-Flash means LLM steering is interesting again
item.description: Ever since Golden Gate Claude I’ve been fascinated with “steering”: the idea that you can guide LLM outputs by directly manipulating the activations of the model mid-flight.

item.title: Announcing the Zulip Foundation
item.description: Today marks a major transition for the Zulip open-source project and for Kandra Labs, the company behind it: I’m stepping back from full-time Zulip leadership to join Anthropic, alongside three senior team members, and we’re donating the company to a newly created, independent, nonprofit Zulip Foundation. …

item.title: ‘No Way To Prevent This,’ Says Only Package Manager Where This Regularly Happens | Kevin Patel
item.description: Kevin Patel - Application Security Engineer @ NISC
item.author: (Kevin Patel)
